label: 1081 to 1086 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 94–265, title IV, § 402(b)90 Stat. 360Apr. 13, 1976
notes: Section 1081, , , ; , , ; 1970 Reorg. Plan No. 4, eff. , 35 F.R. 15627, , dealt with prohibition against foreign fishing within the territorial waters of the United States and conditions under which exceptions to prohibition will be recognized. See sections 1821 to 1825 of this title.Pub. L. 88–308, § 178 Stat. 194Pub. L. 90–42782 Stat. 44584 Stat. 2090May 20, 1964July 26, 1968Oct. 3, 1970

Section 1082, , , ; , , , covered violations and penalties, seizure, forfeiture, and condemnation of property belonging to anyone violating the provisions relating to the territorial waters of the United States. See sections 1857 to 1860 of this title.Pub. L. 88–308, § 278 Stat. 195Pub. L. 91–514, § 184 Stat. 1296May 20, 1964Oct. 27, 1970

Section 1083, , , ; , , ; 1970 Reorg. Plan No. 4, eff. , 35 F.R. 15627, ; , , , related to enforcement responsibilities and joint responsibility of the Secretaries of Commerce, Treasury, and the department in which the Coast Guard is operating. See .Pub. L. 88–308, § 378 Stat. 195Pub. L. 90–578, title IV, § 40282 Stat. 111884 Stat. 2090Pub. L. 91–514, § 284 Stat. 1297section 1861 of this titleMay 20, 1964Oct. 17, 1968Oct. 3, 1970Oct. 27, 1970

Section 1084, , , ; 1970 Reorg. Plan No. 4, eff. , 35 F.R. 15627, , covered authority of Secretaries of Commerce and Treasury to jointly or severally issue regulations under this chapter.Pub. L. 88–308, § 478 Stat. 19684 Stat. 2090May 20, 1964Oct. 3, 1970

Section 1085, , , ; , , , covered definitions of “Continental Shelf fishery resource”, “fisheries”, “Continental Shelf”, and authorized Secretary of Commerce to enlarge list of covered organisms. See .Pub. L. 88–308, § 578 Stat. 196Pub. L. 93–242, § 1587 Stat. 1068section 1802 of this titleMay 20, 1964Jan. 2, 1974

Section 1086, , as added , , , authorized Secretary of the Treasury to pay up to $5,000 to an informer for original information concerning violations under this chapter.Pub. L. 88–308, § 6Pub. L. 91–514, § 384 Stat. 1297Oct. 27, 1970
